rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical amidoselenation of unactivated alkenes with diaryl diselenides via tandem radical selenation/oxidative Ritter reaction to give 1,4-selenoamide products
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of diphenyl diselenide to its radical cation, which fragments into PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe+ is reduced at the cathode to a second PhSe• radical; after radical addition to alkene, the resulting C-radical is re-oxidized at the anode to a selenonium/carbocation; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: selenonium/cyclohexyl cation B (after anodic oxidation of A)
nitrilium ion intermediate C (Ritter adduct with MeCN)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes (PhSe)2 to its radical cation, which fragments to PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe+ is reduced at the cathode to a second PhSe•. PhSe• adds to cyclohexene to give a C-radical A, which the anode oxidizes to selenonium/cation B; MeCN attacks (Ritter) to give nitrilium C, water adds to D, and deprotonation/tautomerization yields the amide 3n while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Water addition to nitrilium intermediate IM3 via TS4 (barrier ~21.12 kcal/mol), the highest barrier on the DFT pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield suppressed to 55% (2 eq) and 20% (4 eq), consistent with radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: 2 eq DPE fully inhibited the reaction (trace product); PhSe-DPE adduct detected by HRMS (m/z 337.0490)

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of selenonium cation
probe_reagents.observation: C12H15Se+ detected (m/z 239.0332), confirming selenonium ion formation

probe_reagents.name: anhydrous/dry MeCN under N2
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 43%, indicating moisture/water is the oxygen source for the amide

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl substitution for (PhSe)2
probe_reagents.observation: No product at 65 °C/5 h; with (NH4)2S2O8 as oxidant 32%; under electrochemical conditions without oxidant 15% — supports radical (not ionic) pathway from diselenide

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclohexene 1n not electroactive in window; (PhSe)2 oxidized at 1.70 V; second oxidation peak of 1n+2n shifts from 2.36 V to 2.10 V upon TsOH addition, showing acid promotes oxidation

probe_reagents.name: Cathodic H2 detection
probe_reagents.observation: H2 evolution confirmed at cathode, consistent with proton reduction completing the redox balance

notes: Authors explicitly disfavor an ionic PhSe+ addition pathway based on PhSeCl control experiments (no reaction without oxidant); the favored pathway is radical addition of PhSe• followed by anodic oxidation of the C-radical (radical-polar crossover) and Ritter trapping by MeCN/H2O. DFT shows the singlet selenonium cation 1IM2 is much more stable than the triplet 3IM2 (-35.93 vs -1.66 kcal/mol), so the singlet cation is the operative species for nucleophilic capture.
